title: Crackt
description: Vein-mining, but fair. Crackt brings progressive destruction to ores - allowing players to mine entire veins without it feeling cheaty and game breaking.
body:
Crackt makes vein-mining balanced and simple, just keep mining a progressively shrinking block and the whole vein will eventually crumble! The goal is to make vein mining feel more vanilla friendly, removing the cheaty-ness of tradition vein miners. 

# How to use
- Install on both the client and server (if playing multiplayer)
- Approach a vein of ore, and mine a block - you'll see it pop right back, but smaller, just continue mining that block until it all comes crumbling down!
- Exceptionally large veins can require up to 24 breaks, and veins can scale up to 128 ores. 
- Multiple players can contribute to the mining progress. 
- All players will see the ore chunk progressively get smaller and smaller. 
- There's no time limit, or anything to worry about - logout, save the game, you can always come back and finish mining. 
- Holding shift allows you mine individual ores, or backout of mining and retrieve the ores you already destroyed by shift breaking the ore chunk block. 
- All enchantments, and durability calculations are 1:1 applied as if you had hand mined the entire chunk, keeping it balanced and mostly vanilla. 
- Developers can include the tag `c:ores` and their modded ores will automatically work with Crackt. 

# Requirements
- Fabric Loader
- Fabric API
- Java 21

---

For tree-felling with a similar concept check out [chopt](https://modrinth.com/mod/chopt)
